content: We can frame naturally occurring biological infectious agents (viruses and bacteria from Topic B) as swarm-configurable ICM curiosity-driven autonomous agents that evolve by intrinsically rewarding themselves for exploring under-characterized host niches, rather than being purely externally driven by selection for replication. This re-framing opens up a new interdisciplinary approach to predicting viral spillover and host switching by borrowing 2025 ICM framework tools for modeling swarm autonomous exploration.
